steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Homo: 'Homo' is a noun meaning 'man' in the vocative

hints.present active indicative: Active would be 'remittunt' — the -untur ending marks passive voice
hints.perfect passive: Perfect passive would be 'remissa sunt' — 'remittuntur' is present passive
hints.imperfect passive: Imperfect passive would be 'remittebantur' — 'remittuntur' is present

hints.mitto, mittere, misi, missum: These are principal parts of 'mitto' (to send) — 'remitto' is the compound
hints.redeo, redire, redii, reditum: These are principal parts of 'redeo' (to return)
hints.reminiscor, reminisci, —, —: These are principal parts of 'reminiscor' (to remember)

hints.nominative: 'Homo' is nominative in form, but here Jesus addresses the paralytic directly — vocative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'hominem' — 'Homo' is vocative (direct address)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'hominis' ends in -is

hints.accusative: Accusative neuter plural is also 'peccata', but here it is the subject of the passive verb
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'peccatis' — 'peccata' is nominative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'peccatorum' — 'peccata' is nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'peccati' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'peccati' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'peccati' ends in -i

translation: Man, your sins are forgiven you
